name: EthCC (Ethereum Community Conference)
location: Palais des Festivals et des Congres, Cannes, France
attendance: ~6,400-6,500 accredited attendees at the main conference; ~10,000 total participants across EthCC Week including ~250-300 parallel/side events. 390-400+ speakers and 64-72 sponsors.
defi_themes: Stablecoins as core financial infrastructure (payments, settlement, lending, cash management) with regulatory clarity (MiCA, US CLARITY/GENIUS context) driving institutional adoption

Institutional DeFi / 'onchain finance' - traditional banks and TradFi firms moving their own business on-chain (first-time official participation by Bloomberg, S&P Global, BNP Paribas, Euroclear, Amundi, Societe Generale-Forge, Tradeweb)
Real-World Asset (RWA) tokenization moving from concept to implementation - tokenized treasuries, RWA-collateralized lending, tokenized stocks/ETFs (dedicated RWA Summit)
DeFi 'renaissance'/revival after ~2 years of contraction, refocused on security, efficiency, interoperability and institutional-grade compliance over short-term yield chasing
Lending innovation - Aave V4 'hub-and-spoke' unified liquidity layer, cross-chain lending, dynamic interest rates, soft liquidation, structured credit and fixed-rate products
AI agents in DeFi (DeFAI) - autonomous agents optimizing liquidity provision, hedging, cross-chain/cross-protocol stablecoin yield and RWA allocation
Restaking / liquid staking integration into lending (Lido, EtherFi, Kelp, Ethena, Lombard as Aave V4 spoke partners)
Tokenized equities and on-chain brokerage (Robinhood L2 on Arbitrum Orbit, 200+ tokenized US stocks/ETFs in EU)
Regulatory frameworks as enabler rather than barrier - shift from 'whether to build' to 'which chain, which products, at what speed'

notable_panels.title: DeFi Summer Day
notable_panels.topic: Dedicated DeFi-focused side event covering DEXs, lending, and DeFi protocol momentum

notable_panels.title: RWA Summit (EthCC 8)
notable_panels.topic: Real-world asset tokenization, tokenized treasuries, compliant DeFi, ETF landscape, regulatory frameworks, institutional onchain infrastructure
notable_panels.speakers: Multiple institutional and protocol speakers (parallel summit)

notable_panels.title: The Agora
notable_panels.topic: Exclusive institutional forum on the future of onchain finance - tokenization, market structure evolution, institutional trading infrastructure; bankers discussing moving their own business on-chain
notable_panels.speakers: Curated by Kaiko; ~600 participants and 60+ expert speakers

notable_panels.title: Aave V4 launch / presentation
notable_panels.topic: Unified hub-and-spoke liquidity layer, cross-chain lending, dynamic interest rates, RWA-collateralized and institutional lending; spoke partners Lido, EtherFi, Kelp, Ethena, Lombard; assets USDT, USDC, EURC, XAUt, cbBTC

notable_panels.title: Stable Summit
notable_panels.topic: Stablecoins and tokenized deposits in cross-border payments and settlement

notable_panels.title: The AI-Powered Financial Stack: Building the Infrastructure of DeFAI
notable_panels.topic: DeFAI adoption barriers, autonomous agent tokenization, ethical design for DeFi+AI
notable_panels.speakers: Moderated by Geng Kai (DFG Head of Research); DeFAI breakfast by Epic Web3, June 29

notable_panels.title: The Onchain AI Stack: Agents, Models, and Infra
notable_panels.topic: Infrastructure scalability, interoperability, agent reliability, verifiable AI for onchain finance
notable_panels.speakers: Moderated by Geng Kai (DFG); Multichain Day, featuring NEAR and ZetaChain

notable_panels.title: To Catch a Token (Robinhood event)
notable_panels.topic: Robinhood Layer 2 (Arbitrum Orbit) and tokenized US stocks/ETFs for Europe

key_takeaways: Institutionalization/professionalization was the dominant narrative - TradFi firms (BNP Paribas, Societe Generale-Forge, Euroclear, Amundi, S&P Global, Bloomberg, Tradeweb) attended as official participants for the first time, signaling a shift from 'what we are building' to 'who is using it and under what compliant framework.'

Stablecoins emerged as the foundational layer of onchain finance - not just a medium of exchange but the base for lending, payments, settlement and RWA, boosted by regulatory clarity (MiCA in EU, US stablecoin/CLARITY momentum) and Circle's NYSE listing.

DeFi is in a 'renaissance' - after ~2 years of contraction, renewed momentum with rising DEX and lending volumes in Q2 2025, now emphasizing security, efficiency, interoperability and institutional-grade compliance over speculative yield.
Aave V4's hub-and-spoke architecture was a marquee DeFi product reveal, pushing unified cross-chain liquidity, RWA-collateralized lending, and dedicated institutional lending environments.
RWA tokenization moved from concept to implementation, with a dedicated RWA Summit and tokenized treasuries/stocks (e.g., Robinhood's tokenized US equities on an Arbitrum L2) taking center stage.
AI agents (DeFAI) emerged as a forward theme - autonomous agents automating liquidity, hedging, and cross-chain stablecoin/RWA yield optimization.
Regulation reframed as an enabler: institutions' question shifted from 'whether' to 'which chain, which products, at what speed.'
confidence: medium-high (dates, edition, location, attendance, and high-level DeFi themes are corroborated by multiple authoritative sources including the official Palais des Festivals press release and EthCC.io; some specific side-event panel titles/speaker attributions rest on single recaps (e.g., DFG) and are labeled accordingly)
